pages.text: The $(2)Custom Mode$() allows a force field of any shape or structure to be projected. A user-defined block pattern is analysed and stored by the $(2)Custom Mode$() item itself, the Projector is able to generate a force field that is an $(9)exact replica$() of the original. Force fields may be constructed in the shape of $(9)anything$() - including a bridge, a house, or even an entire fortress!

pages.title: Usage
pages.text: Use the $(2)Custom Mode$() item on two blocks in the world. These two (primary and secondary) blocks will define a 3D space that the $(2)Custom Mode$() can analyse. As each block is clicked on, their coordinates in the world will be stored as 'Primary pos' and 'Secondary pos'. Use while sneaking to instruct the $(2)Custom Mode$() item to analyze and save all the blocks that exist between the selected points.

The $(2)Custom Mode$() has two modes, additive and subtractive. The additive mode adds blocks to the force field, while the subtractive mode removes blocks. Set the mode by right-clicking air. Note: the additive mode draws in solid geometry, If you draw a large cube, it will be solid! You must ensure hollow space exists where you want it manually!

pages.title: Field Adjustment
pages.text: As with the other projector modes, $(2)Custom Mode$() force fields can be rotated and scaled using the $(l:projector_modules/rotation_module)$(5)Rotation$() and $(l:upgrade_modules/scale_module)$(5)Scale Modules$() respectively.

pages.title: Projection
pages.text: A $(2)Custom Mode$() field being projected with a $(l:projector_modules/camouflage_module)$(5)Camouflage Module$() applied.
